MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Simon for and with Gregory Earles. Jennifer Zarich for and with Margie Earles.

The Court asked Mr. Simon if he had a position regarding the removal of Mr. Earles. Mr. Simon stated that Mr. Earles is willing to nominate his mother and that Mr. Cole's office will take over. The Court stated that an accounting by Mr. Earles will be necessary unless Ms. Earles is willing to waive and that if it is not waived Mr. Simon may be needed to assist Mr. Earles with the accounting. A short recess was taken for Ms. Zarich to confer with her client. Ms. Zarich stated that they are willing to waive the accounting, that the probate examiner questioned Ms. Earles priority to serve, that her nomination by Gregory does that, that Ms. Earles is the sole beneficiary of the estate of Cynthia Earles who was a major beneficiary of Charles Earles and that they have serious time constraints. The Court granted the petition for removal of administrator with will annexed and the motion to be relieved as counsel (subject to review of the order). The petition for letters of administration will annexed was continued to November 2, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the court to analyze the other issues.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Mr. Simon reported that Ms. Zarich will be taking over as attorney and that Margie will take over as administrator. The Court stated that it is suspending Mr. Earles as administrator, that once a substitution of attorney is on file it will take the motion to be relieved as counsel under submission, the order of status hearing is off calendar and a status on filing a petition for successor administrator was set for July 27, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Simon for Gregory Earles. Jennifer Zarich for (beneficiary & administrator of Cynthia Earles estate) Margie Earles.

The Court noted that there is no appearance by Mr. Earles, stated that it has read the papers but without Mr. Earles here is not going to grant the motion to be relieved as counsel. ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-21-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Cole for Margie Lee Earles (Personal Representative for the Cynthia Earles Estate case number 297297). Robert Simon for and with Gregory Earles.

Mr. Simon stated that stated that he just substituted in for Gregory Earles and that he should be getting the file from Mr. Pollak this week. ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-14-2014 Continued To Jan-21-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: Chris Cole for Margie Lee Earles (Beneficiary). Gregory Earles (Administrator).

Mr. Cole reported that Mr. Earles has not met with the attorney that was referred by him. Furthermore, he stated that the related case number is PES-13-297297, that the lender wants to get pay. Court expressed concern with the delay and encouraged Mr. Earles to meet with an attorney promptly. The matter was continued to January 21, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to see if Mr. Earles gets an attorney.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Dec-17-2013 Continued To Jan-14-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Dec-17-2013 Continued To Jan-14-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steve Pollak former counsel for Gregory Earles. Jonathan Ferdon for Public Administrator. Christopher Cole for Margie Lee Earles (Mother of Cynthia - deceased beneficiary). Gregory Earles in Propria Persona.

Mr. Pollak explained that he was contacted by Margie regarding administration of Charles' estate, that he represented Gregory during the early part of administration, that he arranged to continue the foreclosure date, that Margie was in the process of trying to obtain a reverse mortgage and that he then contacted his Brother-in-law to try to get an institutional loan. Mr. Pollak also stated that no one would lend funds, that they got a private loan, that the intent was to refinance after one year, that the Brother-in-law referred the loan out and did not receive a fee for it, and that he (Mr. Pollak) went over to the house after Cynthia passed away. Mr. Cole stated that he can assist with Charles' estate if Gregory agrees on having Margie step in, that it is his understanding that the lender will extend the loan and that they are not hounding to foreclose. In addition, Mr. Pollak stated that he contacted Bank of America regarding the missing $7,000.00 bank account, that he was told that the transaction was done with Gregory's consent, and that he advised Gregory to contact the Police to pursue it. The Court suggested that Gregory speak with Mr. Cole. Gregory stated that he would like to get an attorney. The status hearing was continued to January 14, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If there is no progress, the Court will be inclined to suspend Gregory's powers. Cynthia's probate case number is PES-13-297297.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton. (Not Reported)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Nov-12-2013 Continued To Dec-17-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for Public Administrator. Gregory Earles in Propria Persona.

Mr. Earles stated that he has had no discussion with the former Attorney, that Marjorie owns half of the property, that his Sister passed away since the last hearing, that Marjorie is living in the property, that the loan is due next month and that they will be able to have the loan paid off. Mr. Earles also stated that the property is free and clear except for the $160,000.00 loan and that there is a deed of trust. Mr. Ferdon stated that the Attorney who withdrew needs to be brought in, that he does not feel the Attorney gave the Court the full picture and that there is nothing in the papers regarding the Attorney's involvement with the loan. in addition, Mr. Earles stated that Mr. Pollak came over on Saturday. The status hearing was continued to December 17, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court will issue an order to have Mr. Pollak appear. The Court suggested that Mr. Earles obtain a Lawyer.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Loos. (Not Reported)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Oct-08-2013 Continued To Nov-12-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for Public Administrator. Gregory Earles in Propria Persona.

Mr. Earles stated that he received a check in the amount of $7,000.00 from the Turk Street property, that he deposited the funds at Bank of America on June 25, 2012, that the funds were withdrawn by someone other than himself posing as a relative and that Bank of America stated that he waited to long to file a claim. Mr. Earles also stated that the Vernon Street property was in foreclosure, that they obtained a loan from Mr. Pollak's Cousin to get the house out of foreclosure at 11% percent interest, that the mortgage of $900.00 a month is coming out of the loan funds, that Mr. Pollak has known the Family for about 30-years, and that Mr. Pollak withdrew as Counsel shortly after the loan was obtained. The Order to Appear was continued to November 12, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Pollak to come in.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Sep-04-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steve Pollak for Gregory Earles (Administrator).

The Court noted that Mr. Earles was not present. Mr. Pollak stated that there are a couple of Creditor's Claims that are relatively small, that he has called and written to Mr. Earles, that there has been no cooperation and expressed concern about the Sister's (major Beneficiary) interest. The Court granted the Motion for Withdrawal of Attorney of Record (subject to review of the order). The Court will insert in the order a future hearing date of October 8, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. An Order to Appear will be issued to Mr. Earles to appear on that date and the Public Administrator will also be notified to be present on that date.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Loos. (Not Reported)
